coverage_notes: Strong coverage across all target areas: cadence/step rate (2 papers including foundational Heiderscheit), foot strike (2 papers), vertical oscillation/leg stiffness (2 papers), running economy biomechanics (3 papers including 2 meta-analyses), biofeedback gait retraining (1 paper), and injury risk factors (2 papers). Includes 6 systematic reviews/meta-analyses. Populations span recreational to elite runners. Time range 2011-2024. Gap: no masters-specific biomechanics study found.

papers.title: The Relationship Between Running Biomechanics and Running Economy: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Observational Studies
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 51 studies to quantify associations between running biomechanical variables (cadence, vertical oscillation, leg stiffness, joint angles) and running economy.

papers.title: Biomechanical and Musculoskeletal Measurements as Risk Factors for Running-Related Injury in Non-elite Runners: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Prospective Studies
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 30 prospective studies examining over 100 biomechanical and musculoskeletal measurements as risk factors for running-related injury in non-elite runners.

papers.title: What is the Effect of Changing Running Step Rate on Injury, Performance and Biomechanics? A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 37 studies examining the effects of step rate manipulation on running injury risk, performance, and biomechanics.

papers.title: What are the Benefits and Risks Associated with Changing Foot Strike Pattern During Running? A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Injury, Running Economy, and Biomechanics
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 53 studies examining benefits and risks of changing foot strike pattern on injury, running economy, and biomechanics.

papers.title: Risk Factors for Injuries in Runners: A Systematic Review of Foot Strike Technique and Its Classification at Impact
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 13 studies examining foot strike technique classification methods and their relationship to running-related injuries.

papers.title: Biomechanical risk factors for running-related injury differ by sample population: A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 13 prospective studies demonstrating that biomechanical risk factors for running injury differ depending on the sample population studied.

papers.title: Effects of step rate manipulation on joint mechanics during running
papers.notes: Characterized the biomechanical effects of step rate modification (preferred +/- 5% and 10%) on hip, knee, and ankle joint mechanics in recreational runners.

papers.title: Running Technique is an Important Component of Running Economy and Performance
papers.notes: Measured 19 biomechanical variables in 97 runners of diverse ability to identify which technique variables most strongly correlate with running economy and performance.

papers.title: Is There an Economical Running Technique? A Review of Modifiable Biomechanical Factors Affecting Running Economy
papers.notes: Reviewed modifiable biomechanical factors (stride length, vertical oscillation, ground contact time, leg stiffness, arm swing) and their effects on running economy.

papers.title: Running economy and lower extremity stiffness in endurance runners: A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 13 studies examining the relationship between lower extremity stiffness (vertical stiffness and leg stiffness) and running economy in endurance runners.
